sourceMovie.title: Kneecap
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Kneecap (2024) as a music theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The music and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Commitments
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Kneecap clicked for you, The Commitments (1991) is a strong next stop: both trade in music and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Kneecap?
faq.answer: Try The Commitments, The High Note, Get Rich or Die Tryin'—each shares DNA with Kneecap while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Kneecap?
faq.answer: Kneecap (2024) is best described as Music, Comedy, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Kneecap worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this music lane.
